summary: Rare Monkey & Engineer, strong second‑set blues
blurb: The show delivers a rare electric rendition of “Monkey And The Engineer” plus solid blues highlights such as “How Long Blues”, “Cassidy”, and “Alabama Getaway”. Jerry’s guitar shines on the encore “Not Fade Away”, while Phil lays down tight, melodic bass throughout. Guest spots from Spencer Davis and Bob Dylan add extra excitement.
review: February 12, 1989 at the Great Western LA Forum captured the Dead in a mixed‑bag of moments that still make it worth digging. The first set feels restrained, but the second half bursts into life with the electric surprise of “Monkey And The Engineer”, a handful of bluesy gems (“How Long Blues”, “Dire Wolf”) and a party‑like surge through “Cassidy” and “Alabama Getaway”. Jerry’s lead work is a highlight – his licks on “Not Fade Away” and the driving rhythm on “Alabama Getaway” are praised, even as his vocals stumble on a few numbers. Phil’s bass stays smooth and prominent, especially during “Cassidy”, while Brent’s organ occasionally gets pushed back for guest guitarist Spencer Davis. The drums swing between tight “Drums‑Space” explorations and moments of erratic timing noted by some listeners. Guest appearances from Dylan and Davis give the night an extra layer of historic flavor, making this LA show a memorable snapshot of the band’s late‑‘80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across the seven recordings there are more positive than negative remarks: two reviews are clearly positive, three are mixed and two negative. Positive notes consistently highlight the rare “Monkey And The Engineer”, strong blues numbers, and solid guitar/bass work; negatives focus on occasional vocal slips and erratic drumming. The overall tone leans slightly favorable, warranting a modest +0.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clear mix, includes the rare electric “Monkey And The Engineer” and a solid second set; it’s the most complete and balanced recording despite minor dropouts.

key_highlights: Rare electric performance of “Monkey And The Engineer”.
High‑energy second‑set blues jam featuring “How Long Blues”, “Dire Wolf”, and “Cassidy”.
Jerry’s standout guitar on the encore “Not Fade Away” and “Alabama Getaway”.
Guest appearances by Spencer Davis (guitar) and Bob Dylan add historic excitement.

band_performance.Jerry: Shows flashes of brilliance with soaring leads on “Not Fade Away” and solid rhythm on “Alabama Getaway”, but his vocals slip on several songs, notably “Foolish Heart”.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers consistently smooth, melodic bass throughout; highlighted on “Cassidy” and well‑isolated in the mix.
band_performance.Bob: Mixed reception – some reviewers criticize his stage presence, yet he contributes solid rhythm work and joins guest spots without major issues.
band_performance.Brent: Organ sounds a bit tired on certain tunes and is turned down to accommodate Spencer Davis, but overall provides steady support.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming described as erratic and off‑beat in parts of the show; no standout praise recorded.
